Name: Charizard
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Each of Charizard’s attacks does 10 more damage for each [R] Pokémon on your Bench to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Discard a [R] Energy attached to Charizard.

Name: Froslass
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play Froslass from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may search your deck for any 1 card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Effects: If there is any Stadium Card in play, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It freezes prey by blowing its -58 degrees F breath. It is said to then secretly display its prey.

Name: Heatran
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Discard the top 3 cards of your deck. This attack does 60 damage plus 20 more damage for each [R] or [M] Energy card you discarded.
Flavor-Text: Its body is made of rugged steel. However, it is partially melted in spots because of its own heat.

Name: Kabutops
Effects: You may discard Helix Fossil, Dome Fossil, or Old Amber from your hand. If you do, this attack does 20 damage plus 50 more damage.
Does 10 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It is thought that this Pokémon came onto land because its prey adapted to life on land.

Name: Luxray
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.
If Luxray has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, you may do 100 damage instead of 60 to the Defending Pokémon. If you do, discard that Pokémon Tool card.
Flavor-Text: It can see clearly through walls to track down its prey and seek its lost young.

Name: Mothim
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 in any combination of Burmy and Wormadam, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Seach your discard pile for a basic Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: While it loves floral honey, it won’t gather any itself. Instead, it plots to steal some from Combee.

Name: Probopass
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If you don’t have a Supporter card in play, each of Probopass’s attacks does 30 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Draw a card.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 damage plus 30 more damage.

Name: Salamence
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may reveal the top card of your deck. If that card is a basic Energy card, attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. If that card isn’t a basic Energy card, discard it. This power can’t be used if Salamence is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

Name: Swalot
Effects: Put damage counters on the Defending Pokémon until the Defending Pokémon has the same remaining HP as Swalot. (If the Defending Pokémon has the same or less remaining HP as Swalot, this attack does nothing.)
You may do 50 damage plus 30 more damage. If you do, this attack does 30 damage to 1 of your Pokémon, and don’t apply Weakness and Resistance to this damage.
Flavor-Text: It swallows anything whole. It sweats toxic fluids from its follicles to douse foes.

Name: Tangrowth
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to Tangrowth by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Before doing damage, count the remaining HP of the Defending Pokémon and Tangrowth. If the Defending Pokémon has fewer remaining HP than Tangrowth’s, this attack does 120 damage instead.
Flavor-Text: Its arms are made of plants that bind themselves to things. They grow back right away if cut.

Name: Toxicroak
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
If Toxicroak has any [P] Energy attached to it, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. If Toxicroak has any [F] Energy attached to it, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It has a poison sac at its throat. When it croaks, the stored poison is churned for more potency.

Name: Zapdos G
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, search your discard pile for an Energy card and attach it to Zapdos G.
You may discard all [L] attached to Zapdos G. If you do, this attack’s base damage is 80 instead of 40.

Name: Aerodactyl
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for Helix Fossil, Dome Fossil, or Old Amber, show it your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can’t be used if Aerodactyl is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon that roamed the skies in the dinosaur era. Its teeth are like saw blades.

Name: Bronzong
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. This attack does 40 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
The Retreat Cost for Bronzong is 0 until the end of your next turn.
Flavor-Text: It brought rains by opening portals to another world. It was revered as a bringer of plentiful harvests.

Name: Cherrim
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If any of your [G] Pokémon or [R] Pokémon would be damaged by an attack, reduce that damage by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: If it senses strong sunlight, it opens its folded petals to absorb the sun’s rays with its whole body.

Name: Gengar
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may move 1 damage counter from 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon to another of your opponent’s Pokémon. This power can’t be used if Gengar is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) You may switch Gengar with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The leer that floats in darkness belongs to a Gengar delighting in casting curses on people.

Name: Gengar
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: The leer that floats in darkness belongs to a Gengar delighting in casting curses on people.

Name: Glalie
Effects: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 20 damage plus 50 more damage. Discard that Stadium card.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It prevents prey from escaping by instantaneously freezing moisture in the air.

Name: Golem
Effects: Discard as many [F] Energy cards as you like from your hand. The attack does 30 damage times the number of [F] Energy cards you discarded.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.

Name: Hariyama
Effects: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
If the Defending Pokémon has any Resistance, this attack’s base damage is 120 instead of 60.
Flavor-Text: It loves to match power with big-bodied Pokémon. It can knock a truck flying with its arm thrusts.

Name: Lopunny
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard an Energy card from your hand. If you do, remove 1 damage counter from each of your Pokémon. This power can’t be used if Lopunny is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Remove all Special Conditions from Lopunny.
Flavor-Text: The ears appear to be delicate. If they are touched roughly, it kicks with its graceful legs.

Name: Manectric
Effects: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on the Defending Pokémon.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It discharges electricity from its mane. It creates a thundercloud overhead to drop lightning bolts.

Name: Omastar
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Evolved Pokémon. Remove the highest Stage Evolution card from that Pokémon and have your opponent shuffle that card into his or her deck.
Does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each Helix Fossil, Dome Fossil, and Old Amber in your discard pile.
Flavor-Text: It is thought that this Pokémon became extinct because its spiral shell grew too large.

Name: Pelipper
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is a messenger of the skies, carrying small Pokémon and eggs to safety in its bill.

Name: Pichu
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Pikachu from your hand onto Pichu (this counts as evolving Pichu) and remove all damage counters from Pichu.
Effects: Look at the top 5 cards of your deck, choose 1 of them, and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Flavor-Text: The electric sacs in its cheeks are small. If even a little electricity leaks, it becomes shocked.

Name: Porygon-Z G
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, when you put Porygon-Z G from your hand onto your Bench, you may search your discard pile for up to 2 Pokémon Tool cards, show them to your opponent, and shuffle them into your deck.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 40 more damage.

Name: Raichu
Effects: If Raichu has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon that isn’t an Evolved Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Discard a [L] Energy card attached to Raichu.
Flavor-Text: Its tail discharges electricity into the ground, protecting it from getting shocked.

Name: Rapidash
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Rapidash by your opponent’s Pokémon SP.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 damage plus 20 more damage.

Name: Raticate
Effects: Look at your opponent’s hand, choose a Supporter card you find there, and discard it. Then, use the effect of that card as the effect of this attack.
If Raticate has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 20 damage plus 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It whittles its constantly growing fangs by gnawing on hard things. It can chew apart cinder walls.

Name: Sceptile
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: When you attach a [G] Energy card from your hand to Sceptile, remove 2 damage counters from Sceptile.
Effects: Does 20 damage times the amount of [G] Energy attached to Sceptile.
The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

Name: Sceptile
Effects: You may attach a basic Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 70 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: The leaves that grow on its arms can slice down thick trees. It is without peer in jungle combat.

Name: Spiritomb
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: As long as Spiritomb is your Active Pokémon, each player can’t play any Trainer cards from his or her hand.
Effects: Search your deck for a card that evolves from 1 of your Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon. (This counts as evolving that Pokémon.) If you do, put 1 damage counter on Spiritomb. Shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Bronzong
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It brought rains by opening portals to another world. It was revered as a bringer of plentiful harvests.

Name: Bronzor
Effects: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: There are researchers who believe this Pokémon reflected like a mirror in the distant past.

Name: Charmeleon
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: In the rocky mountains where Charmeleon live, their fiery tails shine at night like stars.

Name: Gastly
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Born from gases, anyone would faint if engulfed by its gaseous body, which contains poison.

Name: Graveler
Effects: Graveler does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It rolls on mountain paths to move. Once it builds momentum, no Pokémon can stop it without difficulty.

Name: Grovyle
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Grovyle during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It lives in dense jungles. While closing in on its prey, it leaps from branch to branch.

Name: Grovyle
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It lives in dense jungles. While closing in on its prey, it leaps from branch to branch.

Name: Gulpin
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, if Gulpin would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, Gulpin is not Knocked Out and its remaining HP becomes 10 instead.
Flavor-Text: Almost all of its body is its stomach. Its harsh digestive juices quickly dissolve anything it swallows.

Name: Haunter
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If Haunter is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if Haunter is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It likes to lurk in the dark and tap shoulders with a gaseous hand. Its touch causes endless shuddering.

Name: Haunter
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It likes to lurk in the dark and tap shoulders with a gaseous hand. Its touch causes endless shuddering.

Name: Luxio
Effects: Discard all [L] Energy attached to Luxio. Flip a coin for each Energy card you discarded. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Strong electricity courses through the tips of its sharp claws. A light scratch causes fainting in foes.

Name: Manectric
Flavor-Text: It discharges electricity from its mane. It creates a thundercloud overhead to drop lightning bolts.

Name: Pelipper
Effects: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each [W] Energy attached to Pelipper.
Flavor-Text: It is a messenger of the skies, carrying small Pokémon and eggs to safety in its bill.

Name: Ponyta
Effects: Search your deck for a card that evolves from Ponyta and put it onto Ponyta. (This counts as evolving Ponyta.) Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: As a newborn, it can barely stand. However, through galloping, its legs are made tougher and faster.

Name: Rapidash
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: When at an all-out gallop, its blazing mane sparkles, enhancing its beautiful appearance.

Name: Shelgon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Shelgon during your opponent’s next turn.
If Shelgon has any basic [R] Energy and any basic [W] Energy attached to it, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Within its rugged shell, its cells have begun changing. The shell peels off the instant it evolves.

Name: Wormadam Plant Cloak
Effects: Remove 2 damage counters from 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
If you have Wormadam Sandy Cloak in play, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned, Confused, and Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: When evolving, its body takes in surrounding materials. As a result, there are many body variations.

Name: Wormadam Sandy Cloak
Effects: If you have Wormadam Trash Cloak in play, this attack does 60 damage plus 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: When evolving, its body takes in surrounding materials. As a result, there are many body variations.

Name: Wormadam Trash Cloak
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. If you have Wormadam Plant Cloak in play, this attack does 40 damage to that Pokémon instead. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
You may search your opponent’s discard pile for any 1 card, show it to your opponent, and put it on top of his or her deck.
Flavor-Text: When evolving, its body takes in surrounding materials. As a result, there are many body variations.

Name: Bagon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: Dreaming of one day flying, it practices by leaping off cliffs every day.

Name: Beedrill G
Effects: If you played Beedrill G from your hand during this turn, this attack’s base damage is 40 instead of 10.
Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

Name: Bronzor
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

Name: Buneary
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon that doesn’t have any damage counters on it. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Its ears are always rolled up. They can be forcefully extended to shatter even a large boulder.

Name: Burmy Plant Cloak
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Burmy Plant Cloak can evolve during the turn you play it.
Flavor-Text: Even if it is born where there are no cocooning materials, it somehow always ends up with a cloak.

Name: Burmy Sandy Cloak
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Burmy Sandy Cloak can evolve during the turn you play it.
Flavor-Text: Even if it is born where there are no cocooning materials, it somehow always ends up with a cloak.

Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Burmy Trash Cloak can evolve during the turn you play it.
Flavor-Text: Even if it is born where there are no cocooning materials, it somehow always ends up with a cloak.

Name: Charmander
Effects: Search your deck for a [R] Basic Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: The fire on the tip of its tail is a measure of its life. If healthy, its tail burns intensely.

Name: Cherubi
Flavor-Text: The small ball is not only filled with nutrients, it is also tasty. Starly try to peck it off.

Name: Croagunk
Effects: Choose 1 card from your opponent’s hand without looking. Look at that card you chose, then have your opponent shuffle that card into his or her deck.
Flavor-Text: It rarely fights fairly, but that is strictly to ensure survival. It is popular as a mascot.

Name: Electrike
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, Electrike does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Using electricity stored in its fur, it stimulates its muscles to heighten its reaction speed.

Name: Electrike
Flavor-Text: Using electricity stored in its fur, it stimulates its muscles to heighten its reaction speed.

Name: Gastly
Flavor-Text: Born from gases, anyone would faint if engulfed by its gaseous body, which contains poison.

Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: At rest, it looks just like a rock. Carelessly stepping on it will make it swing its fists angrily.

Name: Gulpin
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: Almost all of its body is its stomach. Its harsh digestive juices quickly dissolve anything it swallows.

Name: Kabuto
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Evolution cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: It is thought to have inhabited beaches 300 million years ago. It is protected by a stiff shell.

Name: Makuhita
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It toughens its body by slamming into thick trees. Many snapped trees can be found near its nest.

Name: Nosepass
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if Nosepass would be damaged by an attack, prevent that attack’s damage done to Nosepass if that damage is 30 or less.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: When endangered, it may protect itself by raising its magnetism and drawing iron objects to its body.

Name: Omanyte
Effects: Draw 3 cards.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon that was resurrected from a fossil using modern science. It swam in ancient seas.

Name: Pikachu
Effects: Discard all [L] Energy attached to Pikachu and then choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It occasionally uses an electric shock to recharge a fellow Pikachu that is in a weakened state.

Name: Ponyta
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: As a newborn, it can barely stand. However, through galloping, its legs are made tougher and faster.

Name: Rattata
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Cautious in the extreme, its hardy vitality lets it live in any kind of environment.

Name: Shinx
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The extension and contraction of its muscles generates electricity. It glows when in trouble.

Name: Snorunt
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It is said that several Snorunt gather under giant leaves and live together in harmony.

Name: Tangela
Effects: Remove 1 damage counter from Tangela.
The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: The blue vines shrouding its body are covered in a growth of fine hair. It is known to be ticklish.

Name: Treecko
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The soles of its feet are covered by countless tiny spikes, enabling it to walk on walls and ceilings.

Name: Treecko
Flavor-Text: The soles of its feet are covered by countless tiny spikes, enabling it to walk on walls and ceilings.

Name: Wingull
Effects: During your next turn, Wingull can’t use Slashing Strike.
Flavor-Text: It soars high in the sky, riding on updrafts like a glider. It carries food tucked in its bill.

Name: Wingull
Flavor-Text: It soars high in the sky, riding on updrafts like a glider. It carries food tucked in its bill.

Name: Beginning Door
Description: Search your deck for Arceus, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Bench Shield
Description: As long as the Pokémon this card is attached to is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to that Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).
Tool-Rule: Attach Bench Shield to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If that Pokémon is Knocked Out, discard this card.

Name: Buffer Piece
Description: Any damage done to the Pokémon this card is attached to by an opponent’s attack is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance). At the end of your opponent’s turn after you played Buffer Piece, discard Buffer Piece.
Tool-Rule: Attach Buffer Piece to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If that Pokémon is Knocked Out, discard this card.

Name: Department Store Girl
Description: Search your deck for up to 3 Pokémon Tool cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Energy Restore
Description: Flip 3 coins. For each heads, put a basic Energy card from your discard pile into your hand. If you don’t have that many basic Energy cards in your discard pile, put all of them into your hand.

Name: Expert Belt
Description: The Pokémon this card is attached to gets +20 HP and that Pokémon’s attacks do 20 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance). When the Pokémon this card is attached to is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 1 more Prize card.
Tool-Rule: Attach Expert Belt to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If that Pokémon is Knocked Out, discard this card.

Name: Lucky Egg
Description: When the Pokémon this card is attached to is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent’s attack, draw cards until you have 7 cards in your hand.
Tool-Rule: Attach Lucky Egg to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If that Pokémon is Knocked Out, discard this card.

Name: Old Amber
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: As long as Old Amber is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to Old Amber by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).
Description: Play Old Amber as if it were a [C] Basic Pokémon. (Old Amber counts as a Trainer card as well, but if Old Amber is Knocked Out, this counts as a Knocked Out Pokémon.) Old Amber can’t be affected by any Special Conditions and can’t retreat. At any time during your turn before your attack, you may discard Old Amber from play. (This doesn’t count as a Knocked Out Pokémon.)

Name: Professor Oak’s Visit
Description: Draw 3 cards. Then, choose a card from your hand and put it on the bottom of your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Ultimate Zone
Description: During each player’s turn, the player may move an Energy card attached to 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon to his or her Active Arceus as often as he or she likes.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Dome Fossil
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: When you attach a [F] Energy card from your hand to Dome Fossil (excluding effects of attacks or Poké-Powers), search your deck for a card that evolves from Dome Fossil and put it onto Dome Fossil (this counts as evolving Dome Fossil). Shuffle your deck afterward.

Dome Fossil can’t be affected by any Special Conditions and can’t retreat. At any time during your turn before your attack, you may discard Dome Fossil from play. (This doesn’t count as a Knocked Out Pokémon.)

Description: Play Helix Fossil as if it were a [C] Basic Pokémon. (Helix Fossil counts as a Trainer card as well, but if Helix Fossil is Knocked Out, this counts as a Knocked Out Pokémon.) Helix Fossil can’t be affected by any Special Conditions and can’t retreat. At any time during your turn before your attack, you may discard Helix Fossil from play. Arceus can use the attacks of all Arceus you have in play as its own. (You still need the necessary Energy to use each attack.)

Effects: Discard a [L] Energy and a [P] Energy attached to Arceus.
Level-Up-Rule: Put this card onto your Active Arceus. Arceus LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

Name: Gengar LV.X

Name: Salamence LV.X
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), when you put Salamence LV.X from your hand onto your Active Salamence, you may use this power. For each of your opponent’s Pokémon that is Knocked Out by damage from Salamence’s attacks this turn, take 1 more Prize card.
Effects: Discard an Energy card attached to Salamence.
Level-Up-Rule: Put this card onto your Active Salamence. Salamence LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

Name: Tangrowth LV.X
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, remove 4 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon. This power can’t be used if Tangrowth is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Search your discard pile for as many [G] Energy cards as you like and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like.
Level-Up-Rule: Put this card onto your Active Tangrowth. Tangrowth LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

Name: Bagon

Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: As long as Ponyta has any Energy attached to it, Ponyta has no Weakness.
